books.title: The Hidden Staircase
books.author: Carolyn Keene
books.summary: A young amateur detective investigates a supposed haunting.

books.title: Riders of the Purple Sage
books.author: Zane Grey
books.summary: On a ranch in southern Utah, Jane Withersteen refuses to marry a local church elder, and together with her ranch hand and a mysterious gunman she must face retaliatory violence, theft, and murder.

books.title: A Yankee in the Trenches
books.author: Robert Derby Holmes
books.summary: An American describes his experiences in the British Army in the First World War.

books.title: A General History of the Pirates
books.author: Captain Charles Johnson
books.summary: The lives and stories of the daring pirate outlaws who terrorized the Atlantic Ocean and Caribbean Sea in the 18th century.

books.title: Little Lord Fauntleroy
books.author: Frances Hodgson Burnett
books.summary: A poor young boy unexpectedly inherits a peerage and travels to England to live with his grandfather, an Earl.

books.title: The Small Bachelor
books.author: P. G. Wodehouse
books.summary: A struggling artist and his dream girl set off on a series of comedic misadventures.

books.title: Hedda Gabler
books.author: Henrik Ibsen
books.summary: Newly-married Hedda Gabler struggles to control her life and the lives of those around her.

books.title: On a Pincushion
books.author: Mary De Morgan
books.summary: The first collection of fairy tales by Mary De Morgan.

books.title: Piccadilly Jim
books.author: P. G. Wodehouse
books.summary: Jimmy Crocker gets embroiled in convoluted comedic escapades, involving impersonations, kidnapping and theft, on both sides of the Atlantic.

books.title: The King in Yellow
books.author: Robert W. Chambers
books.summary: Ten short stories of madness, hauntings, romance, and art.

books.title: A Personal Record
books.author: Joseph Conrad
books.summary: Conrad recounts his childhood in Poland, his sailing experiences, and the work on his debut novel.

books.title: Ralestone Luck
books.author: Andre Norton
books.summary: The descendants of a noble family, and of pirates, seek to restore their fortunes by locating the “luck” of their family.

books.title: Darkwater
books.author: W. E. B. Du Bois
books.summary: A collection of essays, short fiction, and poetry related to the lives of black people in Jim Crow-era America.

books.title: The Rights of Man
books.author: Thomas Paine
books.summary: A philosopher and political activist presents a forceful argument in favor of the principles of the French Revolution and of republicanism in general, together with a condemnation of monarchy and hereditary power in general.

books.title: Hudibras
books.author: Samuel Butler
books.summary: A knight and his squire set forth for adventure and love.

books.title: Sartor Resartus
books.author: Thomas Carlyle
books.summary: The meta-fictional commentary on the life of “famous” German philosopher Diogenes Teufelsdröckh and his Philosophy of Clothes.

books.title: The Absolute at Large
books.author: Karel Čapek
books.summary: An inventor builds an atomic engine which converts matter into energy, but has the unfortunate side-effect of liberating divinity into the world.

books.title: Fifty-One Tales
books.author: Lord Dunsany
books.summary: A series of otherworldly short stories ranging from the moral to the whimsical.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: George MacDonald
books.summary: A collection of George MacDonald’s fairy tales, short stories, and novellas.

books.title: Three Men in a Boat
books.author: Jerome K. Jerome
books.summary: The humorous travelogue of a boating holiday down the Thames taken by three friends and their dog.
